detail: The structural claim is accurate and verified. apps/fuel/views.py WorkoutPlanListView.post (1190-1206) creates the WorkoutPlan via serializer.save() then calls _expand_plan_workouts() with (a) NO transaction.atomic(), (b) NO idempotency dedup, and (c) the raw plan.schedule_json -- skipping the runtime path's _validate_normalize_schedule. The runtime twin RuntimeWorkoutPlanListCreateView.post (runtime_views.py:1256-1299) was deliberately hardened for exactly these failures: same name+start_date+ACTIVE retry short-circuits (return 200 deduped), create+expand wrapped in one transaction.atomic() with an explicit comment that a mid-loop failure must roll back the plan row, and the schedule is validated/normalized first. The failure mode is real: _expand_plan_workouts does workout_def.get('category', ...) at runtime_views.py:1073, so any schedule_json day value that is not a dict (string/null) raises AttributeError partway through the loop, leaving the plan row plus a partial set of PLANNED Workout/PlanSlot rows committed with no atomicity to undo them. WorkoutPlanSerializer has no validate_schedule_json (validate_detail_json/validate_rpe/validate belong to WorkoutSerializer), so malformed schedules pass DRF validation untouched. With no dedup, a double-submit creates a second full duplicate calendar; the PATCH regen path (1244-1254) does the same unvalidated DELETE-future-PLANNED + re-expand with no atomicity. Context that lowers severity below the claimed 'medium': the reviewer's headline trigger -- a web-UI double-tap -- is NOT currently reachable. /fuel/plans/ is wired at urls.py:118 and is IsAuthenticated, but the Next.js frontend never POSTs to it (no createWorkoutPlan / fuel/plans POST anywhere in frontend/app|lib|components, only finance payoff-plans); product plan creation flows through the hardened runtime/assistant path. So this is a genuine LATENT divergence: the runtime view was explicitly hardened and its IsAuthenticated console twin was left unguarded, inheriting partial-plan and duplicate-calendar risk for any non-frontend caller (iOS app, direct API, future console UI, integration scripts). Real but currently low-blast-radius defect-in-waiting, not an actively-firing user-facing bug.
suggested_fix: Bring WorkoutPlanListView.post/patch to parity with the runtime path: (1) wrap the plan create + _expand_plan_workouts (and the PATCH DELETE+re-expand) in a single transaction.atomic() so a mid-loop failure rolls back the plan row and any partial PLANNED rows; (2) run schedule_json through _validate_normalize_schedule (or add a validate_schedule_json on WorkoutPlanSerializer) before expansion so non-dict day values are rejected with 400 instead of raising AttributeError mid-loop; (3) add the same name+start_date+ACTIVE idempotency short-circuit (return 200 deduped) to guard against double-submit duplicating the calendar. Ideally factor the runtime view's hardened create/regen logic into a shared helper both views call.
evidence: apps/fuel/views.py:1196-1202 plan=serializer.save() then _expand_plan_workouts(...) with no transaction.atomic, no dedup, raw schedule_json
apps/fuel/views.py:1244-1254 PATCH regen DELETEs future PLANNED + re-expands with no validation/atomicity
apps/fuel/runtime_views.py:1252-1292 runtime twin: idempotency short-circuit (return 200 deduped) + transaction.atomic() wrapping create+expand + comment on rolling back the plan row
apps/fuel/runtime_views.py:1231 runtime path validates via _validate_normalize_schedule; consumer path passes raw plan.schedule_json
apps/fuel/runtime_views.py:1073 workout_def.get('category', ...) raises AttributeError on a non-dict schedule value
apps/fuel/serializers.py:39-63 WorkoutPlanSerializer has only create(); no validate_schedule_json (validate_* methods at 113-133 belong to WorkoutSerializer)
apps/fuel/urls.py:118 WorkoutPlanListView wired at /plans/ (IsAuthenticated); no frontend POST to fuel/plans found in frontend/app|lib|components
